content.problemStatement:
Write a program to check if the given number NNN is Armstrong number.

**Note:** Armstrong number is a number that is equal to the sum of its own digits each raised to the power of the number of digits.


Input Format

The first and only line of input contains a single integer, NNN - The number that needs to be checked.


Output Format

Print on a single line:

`YES` if NNN is Armstrong number.
`NO` otherwise.
content.inputFormat: The first and only line of input contains a single integer, N - The number that needs to be checked.
content.outputFormat: Print on a single line: `YES` if N is an Armstrong number, otherwise `NO`.

sampleTestCases.explanation: 153 has 3 digits. 1^3 + 5^3 + 3^3 = 153, so it is an Armstrong number.

solution.approach: To determine if a number is an Armstrong number, we calculate the sum of its digits each raised to the power of the number of digits. If this sum equals the original number, then it is an Armstrong number.
solution.timeComplexity: O(log N) per test case, O(T * log N) total. The precomputation is done once for each test case.
solution.spaceComplexity: O(1) — the dominant structure is a constant amount of space to store the input and the sum of the digits.
